lanes.label: Lane A: MVP Closeout
lanes.goal: Close the internal business MVP with the remaining visible manual proofs.
lanes.done_when: Both remaining manual blockers are passed in the latest MVP gate: device_rollout_verified and vaultwarden_recovery_material_verified.
lanes.next_operator_action: Provide the visible device rollout proof and the fresh offline Vaultwarden recovery proof.
lanes.next_codex_action: Keep all other lanes visible but non-competing, then rerun the MVP gate and regenerate the AI handoff after each new proof.

lanes.label: Lane B: Website/Public Hold
lanes.goal: Keep the public website truth path and blockers visible without pulling the track into active release work.
lanes.done_when: Lane A is closed and the public edge can be resumed with an explicit release decision.
lanes.next_operator_action: No new public release action unless a new security or reachability regression appears.
lanes.next_codex_action: Maintain the truth path, blocker list, and security posture only; do not restart CI or design work.

lanes.label: Lane C: Security/PBS/Infra
lanes.goal: Keep hardening, reapply paths, and audits green without expanding the scope into a full infrastructure program.
lanes.done_when: Lane A is closed and the team intentionally resumes PBS and broader infra certification work.
lanes.next_operator_action: Only act if a security regression or a PBS-critical runtime issue appears.
lanes.next_codex_action: Preserve the audited hardening state and reapply paths; no new certification work before Lane A closes.

lanes.label: Lane D: Stockenweiler
lanes.goal: Keep the support concept, inventory baseline, and remote-access model visible without starting a live rollout.
lanes.done_when: Lane A is closed and Stockenweiler is explicitly promoted into an active support rollout.
lanes.next_operator_action: No live rollout; only provide new facts if real devices or provider details are collected.
lanes.next_codex_action: Maintain inventory and support-model documents only; do not start device migration or WAN admin work.

lanes.label: Lane E: Radio/Media
lanes.goal: Keep radio and media operational without new feature work or UI expansion.
lanes.done_when: Lane A is closed and there is an explicit decision to resume radio/media expansion.
lanes.next_operator_action: Only report breakage that affects current operation.
lanes.next_codex_action: Keep the existing runtime stable and avoid scope growth.

tasks.goal: Franz Surface Laptop and iPhone are visibly accepted as released MVP devices with the required direct entry paths.
tasks.done_when: Franz Surface Laptop and Franz iPhone both have the required direct app entrypoints and the visible everyday path is confirmed.
tasks.next_operator_action: Recover the missing 2FA path blocked by the lost operator smartphone, then walk the Franz Surface Laptop and iPhone through the real direct entrypoints and capture a fresh visible acceptance result.
tasks.next_codex_action: Run scripts/device_rollout_preflight.py first. If it stays ready_for_manual_device_acceptance, use scripts/prove_device_rollout.ps1 with the fresh visible Surface Laptop and iPhone evidence so the MVP gate and AI handoff refresh automatically.

tasks.goal: Vaultwarden recovery material exists offline in two separate physical copies.
tasks.done_when: Two separate offline copies exist and a fresh visible proof is recorded instead of relying on assumption.
tasks.next_operator_action: Create or verify two separate offline copies of the Vaultwarden recovery material and provide a fresh proof.
tasks.next_codex_action: Update the manual MVP check, rerun release_mvp_gate.py, and regenerate AI_SERVER_HANDOFF.md.

tasks.goal: Public website work stays visible as a paused truth path, not as an active release lane.
tasks.done_when: Lane A is closed and public edge work is intentionally reactivated.
tasks.next_operator_action: No action unless a new public regression must be documented.
tasks.next_codex_action: Keep the website gate, blockers, and security notes current; do not resume public release implementation.

tasks.goal: Hardening, PBS status, and infrastructure guardrails remain visible without becoming the active delivery lane.
tasks.done_when: Lane A is closed and a dedicated certification or PBS rebuild block is intentionally resumed.
tasks.next_operator_action: Only escalate if a new regression appears.
tasks.next_codex_action: Maintain the existing audit-green state and the reapply paths; avoid broad new infra work.

tasks.goal: Stockenweiler stays visible as a prepared support track with inventory and remote-access concept only.
tasks.done_when: Lane A is closed and Stockenweiler is explicitly promoted into an active rollout.
tasks.next_operator_action: No live rollout or gateway work; only share new real device facts if available.
tasks.next_codex_action: Keep inventory and support planning current, but do not start the rollout.

tasks.goal: Radio and media remain operational, but expansion work stays out of the active queue.
tasks.done_when: Lane A is closed and there is an explicit decision to expand radio/media again.
tasks.next_operator_action: Only report breakage that impacts current operation.
tasks.next_codex_action: Hold the current operating state; no new features, no new public surface.
